2026-04-20.content:
The maximum document size has been
increased to 16 MiB. To learn more, see [Behavior differences](https://docs.cloud.google.com/firestore/mongodb-compatibility/docs/behavior-differences#documents).

summary.title: Document size limit increased
summary.summary: The maximum document size limit has been increased to 16 MiB. This change allows for larger data storage within documents. For more details on how this impacts behavior, please refer to the documentation on behavior differences.

2026-04-20.content:
Support for text and geospatial search. You
can create text indexes, perform text and geospatial search queries using
the `$text` and `$near` operators, handle language settings, and calculate
relevance scores. To learn more, see [Text search](https://docs.cloud.google.com/firestore/mongodb-compatibility/docs/text-query)
and [Geospatial search](https://docs.cloud.google.com/firestore/mongodb-compatibility/docs/geo-query).

These features are available in [Preview](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages).

summary.title: New Text and Geospatial Search Capabilities
summary.summary: Enhance your applications with powerful text and geospatial search features. You can now create text indexes, perform text and geospatial queries, manage language settings, and calculate relevance scores. These capabilities are available in preview.

summary.title: $lookup now supports let and pipeline
summary.summary: The $lookup aggregation stage now includes support for the 'let' and 'pipeline' options. This enhancement provides more flexibility and power when performing correlated subqueries within your MongoDB aggregations.

2026-04-20.content:
Support for [Change Streams](https://docs.cloud.google.com/firestore/mongodb-compatibility/docs/change-streams).
Change streams let applications access real-time changes (inserts, updates, and deletes) made to a
collection or to an entire database.

This feature is available in [Preview](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages).

summary.title: Real-time Data Change Streams Available
summary.summary: Applications can now access real-time changes like inserts, updates, and deletes to collections or entire databases using Change Streams. This feature is currently in Preview.

2026-04-20.content:
Support for the `drop()` command to delete entire collections.

This feature is available in [Preview](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages).

summary.title: Collection deletion support
summary.summary: You can now delete entire collections using the `drop()` command. This feature is currently in Preview.

2026-04-09.content:
You can now use
Gemini Code Assist to get AI-powered assistance in Firestore
to [generate MQL queries using natural language prompts](https://docs.cloud.google.com/firestore/mongodb-compatibility/docs/write-mql-gemini).
This feature is available in [Preview](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages).

summary.title: AI-powered assistance for Firestore queries
summary.summary: Gemini Code Assist now offers AI-powered assistance for Firestore, allowing you to generate MQL queries using natural language prompts. This new feature, available in Preview, simplifies data querying in Firestore.

2026-03-16.content: Support for [managing TTL indexes](https://cloud.google.com/firestore/mongodb-compatibility/docs/ttl#create_ttl_index) in the MongoDB API.

summary.title: TTL indexes support
summary.summary: You can now manage TTL indexes in the MongoDB API. This feature allows you to automatically remove documents from collections after a specified time. Learn more about how to create TTL indexes.

2026-02-02.content:
The Firestore databases page in the Google Cloud console now includes
a status column. Possible statuses include:

* Ready
* Cloning is in progress
* Restoring from backup is in progress
* Deleted
* Failed

For the cloning and restore statuses, the status column updates upon completion.

summary.title: New status column in Firestore
summary.summary: The Firestore databases page now displays a status column, showing states like Ready, Cloning in progress, Restoring from backup, Deleted, or Failed. This column updates in real-time for cloning and restore operations.

2025-11-21.content: Support for Object as document `_id` identifier.

summary.title: Object ID Support
summary.summary: You can now use objects as document identifiers. This feature enhances flexibility for unique document identification.
